articles.title: Iran war hammers CAE's bottom line, with training centre shutdowns underway
articles.summary: The ongoing Iran war is significantly impacting CAE Inc.'s financial performance, leading the flight simulator maker to scale back its global operations to cut costs. CEO Matthew Bromberg reported a 46% drop in fourth-quarter profit, attributing it to reduced airline flight schedules and pilot training in the conflict zone, as well as soaring jet fuel costs. As part of a "reset year," CAE plans to remove 10% of its commercial flight simulators and shut down four to six training centers to reduce its global footprint by 300,000 square feet.
articles.content: The ongoing Iran war is significantly impacting CAE Inc.'s financial performance, leading the flight simulator maker to scale back its global operations to cut costs. CEO Matthew Bromberg reported a 46% drop in fourth-quarter profit, attributing it to reduced airline flight schedules and pilot training in the conflict zone, as well as soaring jet fuel costs. As part of a "reset year," CAE plans to remove 10% of its commercial flight simulators and shut down four to six training centers to reduce its global footprint by 300,000 square feet.

articles.title: Insider Sell Alert: Wee Cheong Sells Shares of Principal Financi
articles.summary: Wee Cheong, President - Asia & Middle East of Principal Financial Group Inc (PFG), sold 10,745 shares of the company on May 21, 2026, reducing his total holdings to 66,443 shares. This transaction is part of a trend of 8 insider sells versus 1 insider buy for PFG over the past year. With the stock trading at $103.13 and a GF Value of $89.61, the company appears modestly overvalued based on GuruFocus metrics, and its P/E ratio of 14.97 is higher than both the industry median and its historical median.
